response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and past embedded systems experience, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role's critical requirements. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency is listed as required and is entirely absent from the resume. Additionally, their technical background has progressively shifted toward full-stack web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React) over the past five years, moving away from embedded systems, C programming, and security work. The role requires current expertise in Linux kernel modification, security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), and networking protocols—none of which appear in recent roles or demonstrated skill set.
strengths: Proven software craftsmanship with TDD, CI/CD, and quality-focused development practices across multiple senior/staff roles
Early embedded systems experience with C/C++ at National Instruments and foundational Linux administration/infrastructure knowledge
Strong track record of rapid learning across diverse technology stacks and ability to mentor others
concerns: Complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is explicitly required as a native-level qualification
Recent 5+ years focused entirely on web/full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS); last embedded C work was 2014-2015
No demonstrated expertise in security technologies, networking protocols (L2/L3, NETCONF, SNMP), or Linux kernel source code modification

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent C and embedded systems experience
No security or networking technical background
